# Rule: CHANGELOG policy (human + AI traceability)

**All code generated by the Karvey method must be recorded in a CHANGELOG.** The goal is minimal traceability: **who, when and why**, making it explicit that the work was **AI-assisted and with which model**, but **always identifying the responsible human** behind the change.

It applies to ALL code: application (`karvey-impl`), infrastructure as code and pipelines (`karvey-infra`), and deployment scripts (`karvey-deploy`).

## Location

- `CHANGELOG.md` at the root of **each repo** that received code changes (`project.json:repos`).
- If the repo already has a versioning mechanism (see `karvey-impl`: `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, `VERSION`, git tags), the CHANGELOG entry aligns with that version.

## Entry format (mandatory)

*Keep a Changelog* style + traceability block:

```markdown
## [{version}] - {YYYY-MM-DD}

### {Added | Changed | Fixed | Removed}
- {What changed and **why** — functional description} ({change-id})

> 👤 Human owner: {First Last} <{email}>
> 🤖 AI-assisted: {model, e.g. Claude Opus 4.8}
> 🔗 Change: {change-id} · Karvey phase: impl | infra | deploy
```

### Fields
- **version**: project version after the bump.
- **date**: local date (Chile, CLT/CLST) of the change.
- **what + why**: one line per relevant change; the "why" is mandatory, not just the "what".
- **Human owner**: the person directing the work. Obtain from, in order of preference:
  1. `git config user.name` / `git config user.email` of the repo.
  2. The session user, if git is not configured.
  The human can **never** be left empty nor replaced by "AI".
- **AI-assisted**: name of the model in use (e.g. `Claude Opus 4.8`). Makes clear there was AI assistance, without hiding the human.

## Relationship with commits

Consistent with the project's commit convention. When the project uses `Co-Authored-By`, the commit includes the AI co-authorship line, but the **commit author is the human**. The CHANGELOG entry and the commit must tell the same story (who/when/why).

## Verification (gate)

`karvey-qa` (Dimension 6 — Versioning) verifies for each repo with changes:
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` has an entry for the current version.
- [ ] The entry includes the **human owner** (name + contact) and the **AI model**.
- [ ] It includes the "why", not just the "what".
- [ ] The CHANGELOG version matches the project's version file.

If any of these are missing, it is a versioning finding and blocks progress to deploy along with the rest of the gate.
